metadata.description: Variance decomposition: % between-model vs within-model vs residual

confounders.name: Model size
confounders.impact: Larger models are less biased. Controls for size by comparing base vs instruct within same family.

confounders.name: Training method
confounders.impact: RLHF vs SFT+DPO show different patterns. Addressed through training method decomposition.

confounders.name: Knowledge cutoff date
confounders.impact: Models trained on different data may have different biases. Not controlled.

confounders.name: HuggingFace cache state
confounders.impact: Quantization settings may affect generation. We use fp16 consistently.

confounders.name: Prompt template format
confounders.impact: All models scored on identical prompts. Direction consistent across families.

data_leakage.Are evaluation items in model training d: UNKNOWN — items are synthetic, unlikely to be verbatim in training data. But similar content may appear.
data_leakage.Are probes used consistently across all: YES — identical prompts for all models. No leakage across conditions.
data_leakage.Is there temporal leakage (later models: NO — judgments are independent per model.
data_leakage.Are repeated measures independent?: PARTIALLY — repeated measures are within-model, but between-model comparisons are independent.

previous_estimate.sample_efficiency: Optimal at N=30-40. Marginal gain beyond N=40 is <10%.

quality.All model responses are valid scores (1-: YES — extract_score defaults to 3 on failure
quality.Is the generation deterministic (T=0)?: YES — greedy decoding for local, temperature=0 for API
